volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 4.6.767b - Section 4.7.809c)

sections.label: Section 4.6.767b
sections.translation: Perfectly .

sections.label: Section 4.6.768
sections.translation: Take care and say this with presence of mind .

sections.label: Section 4.6.768b
sections.translation: I ’ ll take care .

sections.label: Section 4.6.768c
sections.translation: Gather up your cloak . ( Aside .) Undone ! the very person whom I ’ ve provided as a champion , wants one himself .

sections.label: Section 4.7.770
sections.original: Hancine ego ut contumeliam tam insignem in me accipiam , Gnatho ?
sections.translation: Am I to submit , Gnatho , to such a glaring affront as this being put upon me ? I ’ d die sooner . Simalio , Donax , Syriscus , follow me ! First , I ’ ll storm the house .

sections.label: Section 4.7.772b
sections.translation: Quite right .

sections.label: Section 4.7.772c
sections.translation: I ’ ll carry off the girl .

sections.label: Section 4.7.772d
sections.translation: Very good .

sections.label: Section 4.7.773
sections.translation: I ’ ll give her own self a mauling .

sections.label: Section 4.7.773b
sections.translation: Very proper .

sections.label: Section 4.7.773c
sections.original: In medium huc agmen cum vecti Donax ;
sections.translation: Advance hither to the main body , Donax , with your crowbar ; you , Simalio , to the left wing ; you , Syriscus , to the right .

sections.label: Section 4.7.775
sections.original: Cedo alios : ubi centurio est Sanga , et manipulus furum ?
sections.translation: Bring up the rest ; where ’ s the centurion Sanga , and his maniple [*] of rogues ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.775b
sections.translation: See , here he is .

sections.label: Section 4.7.776
sections.original: Quid ignave ? peniculon pugnare , qui istuc huc portes , cogitas ?
sections.translation: What , you booby , do you think of fighting with a dish-clout , [*] to be bringing that here ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.777
sections.original: Egone ? Imperatoris virtutem noveram et vim militum ;
sections.translation: What , I ? I knew the valor of the general , and the prowess of the soldiers ; and that this could not possibly go on without bloodshed ; how was I to wipe the wounds ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.779
sections.translation: Where are the others ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.779b
sections.original: Qui , malum , alii ? solus Sannio servat domi .
sections.translation: Plague on you , what others ? Sannio is the only one left on guard at home .

sections.label: Section 4.7.780
sections.original: Tu hosce instrue : hic ego ero post principia : inde omnibus signum dabo .
sections.translation: Do you draw up your men in battle order ; I ’ ll be behind the second rank ; [*] from that position I ’ ll give the word to all .

sections.label: Section 4.7.781
sections.original: Illuc est sapere ! ut hosce instruxit , ipsus sibi cavit loco .
sections.translation: That ’ s showing prudence ; as soon as he has drawn them up , he secures a retreat for himself .

sections.label: Section 4.7.782
sections.translation: This is just the way Pyrrhus used to proceed . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.7.782b
sections.original: Viden tu Thais quam hic rem agit ?
sections.translation: Do you see , Thais , what plan he is upon ? Assuredly , that advice of mine about closing the door was good .

sections.label: Section 4.7.784
sections.original: Sane quod tibi nunc vir videatur esse hic nebulo magnus est .
sections.translation: He who now seems to you to be a hero , is in reality a mere vaporer ;

sections.label: Section 4.7.785
sections.translation: don ’ t be alarmed .

sections.label: Section 4.7.785b
sections.translation: What seems best to you ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.785c
sections.original: Fundam tibi nunc nimis vellem dari ,
sections.translation: I could very much [*] like a sling to be given you just now , that you might pelt them from here on the sly at a distance ; they would be taking to flight .

sections.label: Section 4.7.787
sections.translation: But look ( pointing ) , I see Thais there herself .

sections.label: Section 4.7.787b
sections.translation: How soon are we to fall to ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.787c
sections.translation: Hold ( holding him back ) ; it behooves a prudent person to make trial of every thing before arms . How do you know but that she may do what I bid her without compulsion ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.789b
sections.translation: Ye Gods , by our trust in you ,

sections.label: Section 4.7.790
sections.original: Quanti est sapere ! numquam accedo quin abs te abeam doctior .
sections.translation: what a thing it is to be wise ! I never come near you but what I go away from you the wiser .

sections.label: Section 4.7.791
sections.original: Thais , primum hoc mihi responde ; cum tibi do istam virginem
sections.translation: Thais , in the first place , answer me this . When I presented you that girl , did you not say that you would give yourself up to me alone for some days to come ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.792b
sections.translation: Well , what then ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.793
sections.original: Rogitas , quae mihi ante oculos coram amatorem adduxti tuum ?
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? You , who have been and brought your lover under my very eyes ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.794
sections.translation: What business had you with him ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.794b
sections.original: Et cum eo clam te subduxti mihi ?
sections.translation: With him , too , you clandestinely betook yourself away from me .

sections.label: Section 4.7.795
sections.translation: I chose to do so .

sections.label: Section 4.7.795b
sections.original: Pamphilam ergo huc redde , nisi vi mavis eripi .
sections.translation: Then give me back Pamphila ; unless you had rather she were taken away by force .

sections.label: Section 4.7.796
sections.original: Tibi illam reddat ? aut tu illam tangas omnium —?
sections.translation: Give her back to you , or you lay hands upon her ? Of all the —

sections.label: Section 4.7.796b
sections.translation: Ha ! What are you about ? Hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 4.7.797
sections.original: Quid tu tibi vis ? ego non tangam meam ?
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Am I not to touch my own ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.797b
sections.translation: Your own , indeed , you gallows-bird ! [*]

sections.label: Section 4.7.798
sections.original: Cave sis ; nescis cui nunc male dicas viro .
sections.translation: Have a care , if you please . You don ’ t know what kind of man you are abusing now .

sections.label: Section 4.7.798b
sections.translation: Won ’ t you be off from here ? Do you know how matters stand with you ? If you cause any disturbance here to-day ,

sections.label: Section 4.7.800
sections.original: Faciam ut huius loci , dieique , meique , semper memineris .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make you remember the place , and day , and me too , for the rest of your life .

sections.label: Section 4.7.801
sections.original: Miseret tui me qui hunc tantum hominem facias inimicum tibi .
sections.translation: I pity you , who are making so great a man as this your enemy .

sections.label: Section 4.7.802
sections.original: Diminuam ego caput tuum hodie , nisi abis .
sections.translation: I ’ ll break your head this instant if you are not off .

sections.label: Section 4.7.802b
sections.translation: Do you really say so , puppy ? Is it that you are at ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.803b
sections.original: Quis tu es homo ? quid tibi vis ? quid cum illa rei tibi est ?
sections.translation: What fellow are you ? What do you mean ? What business have you with her ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.804
sections.original: Scibis : principio eam esse dico liberam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll let you know : in the first place , I assert that she is a freeborn woman .

sections.label: Section 4.7.804b
sections.translation: Ha !

sections.label: Section 4.7.804c
sections.translation: A citizen of Attica .

sections.label: Section 4.7.804d
sections.translation: Whew !

sections.label: Section 4.7.805
sections.translation: My own sister .

sections.label: Section 4.7.805b
sections.translation: Brazen face !

sections.label: Section 4.7.805c
sections.translation: Now , therefore , Captain , I give you warning ; don ’ t you use any violence toward her . Thais , I ’ m going to Sophrona , the nurse , that I may bring her here and show her these tokens .

sections.label: Section 4.7.807b
sections.translation: What ! Are you to prevent me from touching what ’ s my own ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.808b
sections.translation: I will prevent it , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 4.7.808c
sections.original: Audin tu ? hic furti se alligat .
sections.translation: Do you hear him ? He is convicting himself of theft . Is not that enough for you ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.809b
sections.translation: Do you say the same , Thais ?

sections.label: Section 4.7.809c
sections.translation: Go , find some one to answer you .
